commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads config into bundled channel setup fallback discovery and channel status collection so explicitly disabled bundled setup entries are skipped before generated setup modules load.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +86. Total +103 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can enumerate disabled bundled setup entries during `collectChannelStatus()` because config is not threaded into fallback setup discovery. I did not run a local doctor repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated cleanup can restore `OPENCLAW_LOCALE` after the dynamically imported status tests without changing the runtime fix.

target.title: fix #86039: skip disabled bundled setup fallbacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Disabled bundled channels could still be considered by setup fallback discovery, so doctor/status paths could try to load missing generated setup modules and emit noisy bundled-channel warnings.
- Solution: Thread config into bundled setup fallback discovery and filter explicitly disabled channel/plugin entries before loading setup entries.
- What changed: Updated bundled setup enumeration, setup registry fallback discovery, channel status collection, and focused regression coverage.
- What did NOT change: Runtime bundled channel enumeration for normal active channels, channel transport behavior, or setup feature-specific filtering semantics.

Fixes #86039

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor` should continue cleanly without disabled bundled channel setup warnings when `channels.<id>.enabled=false` and `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled=false` are set for bundled channels such as imessage, irc, mattermost, and signal.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw source checkout `cdaf9fac94` rebased on current `origin/main`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_LOCALE=en \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-86166-evidence/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-86166-evidence/state/openclaw.json \
  env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-86166 \
    node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev doctor --non-interactive
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** OpenClaw doctor terminal output excerpt:
  ```text
  ◇  Plugins ──────╮
  │                │
  │  Loaded: 70    │
  │  Imported: 0   │
  │  Disabled: 55  │
  │  Errors: 0     │
  │                │
  ├────────────────╯
  Run "openclaw --profile dev doctor --fix" to apply changes.
  │
  └  Doctor complete.
  ```

  Disabled-channel warning checks from the captured doctor output:
  ```text
  failed to load bundled channel irc: False
  failed to load bundled channel setup entry irc: False
  failed to load bundled channel imessage: False
  failed to load bundled channel setup entry imessage: False
  failed to load bundled channel mattermost: False
  failed to load bundled channel signal: False
  Doctor complete: True
  Plugin errors zero: True
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The real doctor run completed and did not emit the disabled bundled-channel warning strings for imessage, irc, mattermost, or signal.
- **What was not tested:** A packaged Windows incomplete-build install was not available in this Linux environment.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Real local doctor run, focused channel setup/status regression tests, and changed-file typecheck/lint gates.
- Target test file: `src/channels/plugins/bundled.shape-guard.test.ts`, `src/flows/channel-setup.status.test.ts`, `src/flows/channel-setup.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: Disabled bundled setup plugins are filtered using config before generated setup entries are loaded, and channel status passes config into fallback setup plugin discovery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact fallback boundary that emitted disabled-channel setup warnings without changing active channel loading.
- Commands run:
  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_LOCALE=en LANG=C.UTF-8 pnpm exec vitest run \
    test/scripts/crabbox-wrapper.test.ts \
    src/flows/channel-setup.status.test.ts \
    src/channels/plugins/bundled.shape-guard.test.ts \
    --reporter=dot

  OPENCLAW_LOCALE=en LANG=C.UTF-8 pnpm exec vitest run \
    src/flows/channel-setup.test.ts \
    src/flows/channel-setup.status.test.ts \
    src/channels/plugins/bundled.shape-guard.test.ts \
    --reporter=dot

  pnpm check:changed
  ```
- Results:
  ```text
  Test Files  3 passed (3)
  Tests       152 passed (152)

  Test Files  3 passed (3)
  Tests       53 passed (53)

  [check:changed] typecheck core
  [check:changed] typecheck core tests
  [check:changed] typecheck extensions
  [check:changed] typecheck extension tests
  [check:changed] lint core
  [check:changed] lint extensions
  runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
  Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
  ```
- Broader local note: `pnpm test:changed` selected unrelated unit-fast tests outside this PR diff and failed on local/base issues; the PR-diff tests and `pnpm check:changed` passed on the current head.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Broad setup fallback discovery listed every bundled setup plugin without the user's config, while the disabled-channel filter was only applied to feature-specific setup helpers.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression proving disabled bundled channels are skipped before setup-entry loading in fallback setup discovery.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
